text:
In software development, Git branching is essential for managing code changes. A common workflow involves creating separate feature branches for each new task. For example, while the main branch remains stable for releases, a developer can create a new branch to work on a "login page" feature. This keeps the main code safe from experimental or incomplete work.

Once the feature is complete and tested, it's time for merging. The developer submits a pull request, which is a request to combine their feature branch with the main branch. The team can review the code, suggest improvements, and discuss changes before merging. This process creates a clear version control workflow, ensuring that only polished and approved code becomes part of the project.
